title: Palindrome Number
description:
Palindrome Number

Given an integer x, return true if x is a palindrome, and false otherwise.

An integer is a palindrome when it reads the same forward and backward.

Examples:

  • Input: x = 121
    Output: true
    Explanation: 121 reads as 121 from left to right and from right to left.
  • Input: x = -121
    Output: false
    Explanation: From left to right, it reads -121. From right to left, it reads 121-. Therefore it is not a palindrome.
  • Input: x = 10
    Output: false
    Explanation: Reads 01 from right to left. Therefore it is not a palindrome.

Constraints:

  • -231 <= x <= 231 - 1

Follow up: Could you solve it without converting the integer to a string?
solution_text:
Palindrome Number

Algorithm (reverse half the number):

  1. Negative numbers and numbers ending in 0 (except 0 itself) are never palindromes
  2. Reverse only the second half of the digits to avoid overflow
  3. Stop when the reversed half is >= the remaining first half
  4. Compare: for even-digit numbers check equality; for odd-digit numbers, divide the reversed half by 10

Time Complexity: O(log n) — we process roughly half the digits

Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights:

  • Negative numbers always fail (the minus sign breaks symmetry)
  • Any positive number ending in 0 (except 0 itself) cannot be a palindrome since no number starts with 0
  • Reversing only half the number avoids integer overflow entirely
